items.title: Abstract Factory Pattern
items.description: Explains the Abstract Factory pattern for creating related object families without coupling code to concrete classes.

items.title: Adapter Pattern
items.description: Explains the Adapter pattern for bridging incompatible interfaces so existing classes can work together cleanly.

items.title: Bridge Pattern
items.description: Explains the Bridge pattern for separating abstractions from implementations so both can evolve independently.

items.title: Builder Pattern
items.description: Explains the Builder pattern for constructing complex objects step by step with clearer, more maintainable APIs.

items.title: Chain of Responsibility Pattern
items.description: Explains the Chain of Responsibility pattern for passing requests through a handler pipeline until one handles them.

items.title: Command Pattern
items.description: Explains the Command pattern for encapsulating requests as objects to support queuing, logging, and undo operations.

items.title: Common OS Interview Questions
items.description: Comprehensive collection of operating systems interview questions with detailed answers, covering all major topics from processes to Linux internals.

items.title: Composite Pattern
items.description: Explains the Composite pattern for modeling part-whole hierarchies and treating single objects and object groups uniformly.

items.title: Decorator Pattern
items.description: Explains the Decorator pattern for adding responsibilities to objects dynamically without relying on subclassing.

items.title: Facade Pattern
items.description: Explains the Facade pattern for exposing a simple interface over complex subsystems to reduce coupling.

items.title: Factory Method Pattern
items.description: Explains the Factory Method pattern for deferring object creation to subclasses while preserving a common creation contract.

items.title: Java Collections Framework: Deep Dive
items.description: Deep dive into the Java Collections Framework, including core interfaces, implementations, performance traits, and best practices.

items.title: Java Concurrency: Threads, Locks & Concurrent Utilities
items.description: Comprehensive guide to Java concurrency, covering threads, synchronization, locks, concurrent utilities, and async programming.

items.title: Java Design Patterns: Overview
items.description: Overview of Java design patterns, their categories, and how they improve maintainability, readability, and software design decisions.

items.title: Java Fundamentals: Core Language Concepts
items.description: Covers Java fundamentals, including the platform model, syntax, types, control flow, and essential language concepts.

items.title: Java I/O: Streams, NIO & I/O Models
items.description: Guide to Java I/O, including stream APIs, NIO channels and buffers, file handling, and I/O model fundamentals.

items.title: Java Interview Questions & Answers
items.description: Curated Java interview questions and answers spanning core language topics, JVM internals, collections, concurrency, and OOP.

items.title: Java Knowledge Base Overview
items.description: A comprehensive reference covering Java fundamentals, collections, concurrency, JVM internals, modern language features, and interview preparation.

items.title: Java New Features: Java 8 through Java 21+
items.description: Practical guide to modern Java features introduced from Java 8 through Java 21, with emphasis on high-impact language updates.

items.title: JVM Internals: Memory, GC & Class Loading
items.description: Guide to JVM internals covering memory layout, garbage collection, class loading, and runtime performance basics.

items.title: Memory Management
items.description: Virtual memory, paging, segmentation, TLB, page replacement algorithms, and Java heap management with GC fundamentals.

items.title: Networking & IPC
items.description: Network stack internals, socket programming, TCP deep dive, IPC mechanisms, and high-performance networking with Java NIO and Netty patterns.

items.title: Object-Oriented Programming in Java
items.description: Guide to object-oriented programming in Java, covering encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction, and SOLID principles.

items.title: Observer Pattern
items.description: Explains the Observer pattern for propagating state changes to dependent objects through event-style notifications.

items.title: Operating Systems Knowledge Base
items.description: A comprehensive knowledge base covering all major operating system concepts, with a focus on Linux internals and Java/JVM perspective.

items.title: Processes & Threads
items.description: Deep dive into processes, threads, their lifecycle, memory layout, context switching, and Java/JVM perspective on concurrency primitives.

items.title: Prototype Pattern
items.description: Explains the Prototype pattern for creating new objects by cloning existing instances instead of rebuilding them from scratch.

items.title: Proxy Pattern
items.description: Explains the Proxy pattern for controlling access to another object through an intermediary with the same interface.

items.title: Singleton Pattern
items.description: Explains the Singleton pattern for ensuring a single shared instance and a controlled global access point.

items.title: Socket Programming & I/O Models
items.description: Blocking vs non-blocking I/O, Java NIO, epoll, the C10K problem, Netty, and how event-driven servers handle massive concurrency.

items.title: Spring Boot — Advanced Topics
items.description: Advanced Spring Boot topics covering security, performance tuning, reactive systems, deployment, and production-ready design.

items.title: Spring Boot — Internals & Architecture
items.description: Deep dive into Spring Boot internals, including auto-configuration, embedded servers, conditional beans, starters, and events.

items.title: Spring Boot — Overview & Why It Matters
items.description: Overview of Spring Boot, its core benefits, and why it is widely used for modern Java backend and microservice development.

items.title: Spring Boot: Interview Questions
items.description: Curated Spring Boot interview questions and answers spanning fundamentals, advanced topics, and real-world production scenarios.

items.title: Spring Data JPA — Complete Guide
items.description: Complete guide to Spring Data JPA, including repositories, entities, query methods, and persistence best practices.

items.title: Spring Framework: Deep Dive
items.description: Advanced Spring Framework guide covering bean lifecycle, AOP, data access, reactive programming, and batch processing.

items.title: Spring Framework: Interview Questions
items.description: Collection of Spring Framework interview questions and answers covering core concepts, containers, web, data, and architecture topics.

items.title: Spring Framework: Overview
items.description: Foundational overview of the Spring Framework, including IoC, dependency injection, modules, and enterprise application development.

items.title: Spring MVC — Complete Guide
items.description: Complete guide to Spring MVC covering controllers, request handling, model binding, validation, and REST application structure.

items.title: Spring Security — Complete Guide
items.description: Comprehensive guide to Spring Security, including authentication, authorization, filter chains, and common application security patterns.

items.title: Strategy Pattern
items.description: Explains the Strategy pattern for swapping algorithms at runtime through a shared interface and composition.

items.title: Synchronization & Deadlocks
items.description: Race conditions, critical sections, mutexes, semaphores, monitors, classic synchronization problems, deadlock detection/prevention, and Java concurrency utilities.

items.title: Template Method Pattern
items.description: Explains the Template Method pattern for defining an algorithm skeleton while allowing subclasses to customize specific steps.
